behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module-to-provider handoff: request builder + API client wiring can drop labels even if a pure formatter unit test passes. A fake/in-process provider that captures outbound payload is sufficient. Rejected System because behavior text only requires request content correctness, not real network/OS/subprocess behavior. Rejected Agentic because no dependence on model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run classifier end-to-end in-process with a stub provider transport that records the actual outbound request. Assert the taxonomy field contains every configured valid label for multiple taxonomy sizes/orderings.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is response-handoff safety: provider adapter response + normalization/validation path. A mocked provider response with an out-of-taxonomy label can expose forwarding bugs at module boundaries. Rejected Agentic because behavior text fixes the provider output to an invalid label; no real model judgment is under test. Rejected System because real HTTP is not required to reproduce this mapping failure.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process provider stub returning a syntactically valid response with label `"nonsense"`. Call public classify API and assert returned value is exactly `"unknown"` and raw label is not leaked.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is pure deterministic normalization logic on identical input; no module handoff, I/O, or model variability is required. Higher levels add cost without increasing failure detection for this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function/object repeatedly with the same raw response fixture (including casing/whitespace variants encoded in that fixture set) and assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on repeated live AI calls and consistency of real model/provider outputs over time. A stub would hide nondeterminism/drift and always pass. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification, not a multi-agentic pipeline with staged decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute N real classification calls against the live provider for identical document+taxonomy under controlled prompt/config; record provider raw responses and final labels. Assert label stability when raw provider output is unchanged, and only allow label change when raw output differs.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at config→budgeting→dispatch handoff; unit-testing token math alone can miss that oversized requests are still dispatched. In-process wiring with a dispatch spy catches real boundary failure. Rejected System because enforcement is explicitly before dispatch, so real network is unnecessary. Rejected Agentic because no reliance on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small token budget, invoke classify with over-budget document, and use a stub dispatcher to assert policy outcome: either truncated payload size within limit or request rejected with policy error, and no over-budget outbound request occurs.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Requires real provider call and real provider-reported usage accounting. Mock usage would trivially pass and hide true over-budget behavior/cost drift. Rejected Integration because correctness depends on real AI API behavior, not only local orchestration. Rejected Workflow because this is a single call budget check, not multi-step agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run live classification on representative documents and assert provider-reported total token usage metadata is <= configured budget per call; track failures by model/version to detect drift.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is retry orchestration across modules (error handling, backoff policy, retry loop). A deterministic stub that returns rate-limit then success reproduces the failure without real network. Rejected System because real HTTP timing is not required for correctness of retry decision path. Rejected Agentic because outcome does not depend on model content/quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use provider stub sequence: first response is rate-limit error, second is success. Assert classifier waits according to policy (or injected clock), performs retry, and returns successful label instead of surfacing first error.
